summary: Build fails with 28 TS errors, 14 tests fail. Core issue: threadId→channelId rename was mechanically incomplete — function parameters/types were renamed but many references in function bodies still use the old name (or vice versa), causing undefined variable errors. Additionally, SessionRow type fields were renamed (thread_id→channel_id) but the actual DB columns are still thread_id, causing SQL parameter mismatches at runtime. New channel-service.ts itself is well-implemented.

issues.description: Variable renamed to channelId (line 20) but lines 35, 47, 53-56 still reference undeclared threadId. Will crash at runtime.
parentChannelId fallback uses threadId (line 101: `rootSession?.channelId ?? threadId`), but threadId was renamed to channelId on line 26.
deleteTask(): line 295 `client.channels.fetch(threadId)` and line 302 response includes `threadId` — but variable was renamed to channelId on line 273. archiveTask() has same issue at line 322.
`const channelId = channelId` — self-assignment (variable used before declaration). Also line 65 references undeclared threadId in fallback name.
parentChannelId fallback uses threadId (line 107: `rootSession?.channelId ?? threadId`). Multiple other threadId references at lines 276 (messageQueue.sendLong).
messageQueue.sendLong(threadId, ...) at line 157 — threadId undefined. parentChannelId fallback at line 178 uses threadId.
handleAttach(): threadName fallback uses threadId (line 235). holder.channelId === threadId comparison (line 247) — threadId is undefined.
channelName fallback uses threadId (line 335: `channel-${threadId}`) — threadId undefined.
ChannelUpdate event: newChannel type is DMChannel|NonThreadGuildBasedChannel; DMChannel has no guild property. Need type guard (check type !== ChannelType.DM first). Lines 203, 215.
handleGoalDrivePrompt: line 548 messageQueue.sendLong(threadId,...) — threadId is undefined (renamed to channelId at 512). Same at lines 625, 665.
guilds.fetch(guildId) — guildId is potentially undefined here (from getAuthorizedGuildId). Type error: string | undefined not assignable to string | FetchGuildOptions.

Massive incomplete rename: parameter renamed to channelId but ~40 references in function body still use threadId (lines 91, 121, 127, 134, 140, 152, 179, 184, 209, 255, 276, 322, 337, 342, 348, 436, 498, 516, 600, 606, 613, 625, 641, 661, etc.).
sendChatInternal passes `channelId` property to claudeClient.chat() options, but the ClaudeExecutor options type doesn't have channelId field (TS2353).
sendChatByIds/handleBackgroundChat: parameter name is still threadId but body references channelId (line 683). Reverse of the usual bug.
findWaitingCustomText: compares entry.channelId but PendingEntry type doesn't have channelId property (has threadId).
detachAll: constructs registry entry with `channelId: active.channelId` but ActiveProcess type has threadId, not channelId. Lines 856, 875, 897 in reconnectAll have same issue.

sessionToParams() outputs key `channel_id` but SQL upsert statement uses @thread_id parameter binding. Runtime error: 'Missing named parameter thread_id'. Same issue with parent_channel_id vs @parent_thread_id. Also affects restore() at lines 260, 270.
rowsToGoalDriveState: references goal.drive_thread_id but GoalRow type renamed field to drive_channel_id. Similarly line 248: t.thread_id but GoalTaskRow renamed to channel_id.

SessionRow.thread_id renamed to channel_id, but actual DB column is still thread_id. GoalRow.drive_thread_id→drive_channel_id and GoalTaskRow.thread_id→channel_id have same issue. Type should match DB column names, not logical names.
startRefixPipeline: parameter is channelId but body calls runAudit/auditFixLoop with threadId (undefined). Lines 337, 343, 405.
executeTaskInBackground: parameter is threadId but body uses channelId (undefined) at line 1057. Reverse direction bug.
pipelineResearch/pipelineSimpleCode/pipelineComplexCode/auditFixLoop/runAudit: parameter is threadId but body uses channelId (undefined). Affects ~20 call sites across lines 1176-1413.
makeState(): goalSeq is optional (number | undefined) but GoalDriveState requires number. Type mismatch.
messageQueue.sendLong(threadId, ...) in commit error handler — even after fix, should this be channelId? Ensure error messages go to the correct channel.
syncFromDiscord uses hardcoded default cwd '/default' for channels without existing records. Consider using the guild's default working directory instead.
